name: Veterans & Civilian Credit Restoration Corp
description_short: Credit restoration company serving veterans and civilians offering dispute services, debt negotiation, and credit counseling with a 90-day results guarantee or money back.
description_long:
Veterans and Civilian Credit Restoration is a credit repair firm positioned to serve both military service members and civilian consumers seeking to improve their credit profiles. The company operates through its website at veteransandciviliancreditrestoration.com and advertises specialized focus on post-military financial recovery alongside general credit repair services.

The company offers credit dispute services, debt negotiation and counseling, identity theft credit recovery, mortgage credit repair, and claims to help remove bankruptcies, charge-offs, late payments, collections, and other negative items from credit reports. They market a 90-day results guarantee with money-back assurance and claim to help clients recover up to $4,000 from credit bureaus through credit deletions. The company provides consultation packages ranging from free 15-minute sessions to $99.99 one-hour financial roadmap sessions, along with downloadable e-books priced at $29.99 each covering bankruptcy removal and credit bureau litigation.

The company differentiates itself through targeted marketing toward veterans combined with civilian services, and promotes having completed 854+ cases with 552+ happy customers. They offer multiple service tiers and emphasize personalized strategies for credit rebuilding. Contact information provided includes a phone number (619-270-7053) for consultation booking and pricing inquiries.

Critical assessment reveals several concerns: the website contains incomplete FAQ answers (multiple sections with placeholder Lorem Ipsum text), making it difficult to understand actual processes or timelines. Claims about removing bankruptcies in 60 days and winning $4,000 from credit bureaus through litigation are presented aggressively without legal disclaimers typical of compliant credit repair firms. The money-back guarantee and specific timelines lack detailed terms. No visible information about FCRA compliance, business licensing, or registration with state attorneys general is provided on the site.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Offers free 15-minute initial consultation to evaluate credit situation
Multiple service tiers available ($0–$99.99) to accommodate different budgets
Positions itself as specialist for veteran credit issues alongside civilian services
Claims 90-day results guarantee with money-back promise
Provides supplemental educational e-books ($29.99) on bankruptcy and credit bureau disputes
Lists specific negative items they claim to address: bankruptcies, charge-offs, late payments, collections
Advertises debt negotiation and identity theft recovery services beyond dispute-only model
cons: Website contains incomplete FAQ section with placeholder Lorem Ipsum text, suggesting unprofessional maintenance
Makes aggressive claims about 60-day bankruptcy removal and $4,000 credit bureau settlements without visible legal disclaimers or FCRA compliance information
No transparent disclosure of guarantee terms, conditions, or limitations despite prominent 90-day money-back claim
No visible business licensing, registration status, or state attorney general information provided on website
Lacks specific case studies, client testimonials with verification, or detailed process explanations beyond broad service categories
best_for: Veterans transitioning to civilian life seeking credit recovery after service-related financial hardship
Consumers with multiple negative items (collections, charge-offs, late payments) willing to pay for dispute services
People interested in debt negotiation alongside credit repair and seeking bundled financial guidance
diagnosis: Best for veterans and civilians seeking aggressive credit dispute services with debt negotiation bundled in, particularly those willing to pay consultation fees. Major caveat: the website's incomplete FAQ sections, lack of regulatory disclosures, and unsubstantiated claims about bankruptcy removal timelines and settlement amounts raise red flags about transparency and FCRA compliance; verify licensing and guarantees before engaging.
services: Civilian credit repair with focus on report inaccuracies
Mortgage credit repair for loan approval improvement
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
